data.title: Bowhead Specialty prices secondary offering at $29 per share
data.description:
• Bowhead Specialty (NYSE:BOW) priced an underwritten secondary public offering of 4M shares at $29.00 per share.
  • Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 600,000 shares.
  • The company is not selling any shares of its common stock in the offering and will not receive any of the proceeds from the sale.
  • The offering is expected to close on October 25, 2024.
  • The stock price gained 1.7% on Wednesday during after hours of trading.

data.title: Mercer International prices $200M senior notes
data.description:
• Mercer International (NASDAQ:MERC) priced its private offering of $200M in aggregate principal amount of 12.875% senior notes due October 1, 2028.
  • The additional notes are being issued at a price of 103.000% of their principal amount, plus accrued interest from October 1, 2024, for a yield to worst of 11.624%.
  • The net proceeds, along with cash on hand, will be used by the company to redeem $300M in principal amount of its currently outstanding 5.500% senior notes due 2026.

data.title: Asian markets take a dip after Wall Street slides amid an earnings flurry
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly lower on Thursday after the Wall Street plunged the previous day, with 9 out of 11 S&P sectors finished in red.

Japan (NKY:IND) +0.02% Japan's flash PMI numbers show fastest output decline in six months.

The Japanese yen hovers at its weakest levels in nearly three months amid political uncertainty ahead of the country’s general election this weekend.

Polls suggested that the coalition government could lose its majority in parliament, clouding the trajectory of Bank of Japan interest rate hikes.

China (SHCOMP) -0.50% with mainland stocks snapping a four-day advance as the upcoming US presidential election kept investors on edge for a potential escalation of Sino-US tensions.

Hong Kong (HSI) -0.99% after gaining in the prior two sessions, weighed by steep declines in most sectors.

The annual inflation rate in Malaysia edged down to 1.8% in September 2024. Food prices in Malaysia increased by 1.6% Y/Y in September.

India (SENSEX) -0.16%

Australia (AS51) +0.11% hitting a two-week low and tracking losses on Wall Street overnight as rising US Treasury yields and uncertainty surrounding Federal Reserve rate cuts weighed on equities. Australia's flash PMI estimates shows declining business activity

In the U.S., on Wednesday, all three major indexes ended lower, weighed down by a slide in technology and consumer discretionary stocks and a post-earnings plunge.

U.S. stock futures were mixed on Thursday as investors remain concerned about earnings results: Dow -0.14%; S&P 500 +0.20%; Nasdaq +0.50%.

Investors now look ahead to more earnings on Thursday from UPS, American Airlines and Northrop Grumman, among others

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: Australia's flash PMI estimates shows declining business activity
data.description:
The Judo Bank Australia Manufacturing PMI edged lower to 46.6 in October 2024 from 46.7 in September, a 53-month low, flash readings showed. This indicates a deterioration in the sector's health for the ninth consecutive month and at the fastest rate since May 2020.

Flash Services PMI Business Activity Index edged up to 50.6 in October from 50.5 in September, according to preliminary estimates. It was the ninth consecutive month of expansion.

Composite PMI Output Index inched higher to 49.8 in October, from 49.6 in September, flash readings showed. This indicates a second consecutive month of declining private sector output, though the decrease was marginal and less pronounced than before.

The Australian dollar steadied around $0.664 on Thursday, but traded close to its lowest levels in two months as the greenback and Treasury yields rallied

ETFs: (EWA), (FXA), (FLAU).

Currencies: (AUD:USD), (USD:AUD)

data.title: Japan's flash PMI numbers show fastest output decline in six months
data.description:
The au Jibun Bank Japan Manufacturing PMI declined to 49.5 in October 2024 from a final 49.7 in the previous month, below-market forecasts of 49.9, marking the fourth straight month of contraction in factory activity.

This marked the eighth consecutive month of decline in the manufacturing sector this year, and the strongest contraction since March, as output and new orders shrank the most in six and seven months, respectively, with faster declines in export orders.

Japan's flash services PMI slipped to 49.3 in October 2024 from a final 53.1 in the prior month. It was the first drop in the service sector since June and the steepest pace since February 2022, as there was a renewed reduction in new business in the face of the economic weakness both a domestic and international level.

Flash Composite PMI fell to 49.4 in October 2024 from a final 52.0 in September, pointing to the lowest reading since November 2022. It was also the first contraction in private sector activity since June, as the manufacturing sector deteriorated further and the service economy shrank for the first time in four months.

The Japanese yen traded around 152.4 per dollar on Thursday after losing over 1% in the previous session, hovering at its weakest levels in nearly three months amid political uncertainty ahead of the country’s general election this weekend.

Currencies: (JPY:USD) (USD:JPY)

ETFs: (EWJ), (JEQ), (DXJ), (FXY), (NKY:IND)

data.title: Equinor Non-GAAP EPS of $0.79, revenue of $25.44B beats by $300M
data.description:
• Equinor press release (NYSE:EQNR): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.79.
  • Revenue of $25.44B (-2.2% Y/Y) beats by $300M.
  • Equinor delivered a total equity production of1,984 mboe per day in the third quarter, downfrom 2,007 mboe in the same quarter last year
  • Outlook
  • Organic capital expenditures are estimated atUSD 12-13 billion for 2024.
  • Oil & gas production for 2024 is estimated to bestable compared to the 2023 level
  • Renewable power generation for 2024 isestimated to increase by around fifty percentcompared to the 2023 level.
  • Equinor’s ambition is to keep the unit of productioncost in the top quartile of its peer group
  • Scheduled maintenance activity is estimated toreduce equity production by around 50 mboe perday for the full year of 2024.

data.title: Spirit AeroSystems Non-GAAP EPS of -$3.03, revenue of $1.5B misses by $400M
data.description:
• Spirit AeroSystems press release (NYSE:SPR): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS may not be comparable to consensus of $0.15.
  • Revenue of $1.47B (+2% Y/Y) misses by $430M.
  • Spirit's revenue in the third quarter of 2024 increased from the same period of 2023, primarily due to higher production activities on most Commercial programs and higher Defense and Space revenues, partially offset by lower production volume on the Boeing 737 program.
  • Overall deliveries were consistent in the third quarters of 2024 and 2023, with 332 shipsets delivered in both periods.
  • Spirit's backlog at the end of the third quarter of 2024 was approximately $48 billion, which includes work packages on all commercial platforms in the Airbus and Boeing backlog.
  • Shares -4% PM.

data.title: Teck Resources Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.60, revenue of C$2.86B
data.description:
• Teck Resources press release (NYSE:TECK): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.60.
  • Revenue of C$2.86B (+43.7% Y/Y).
  • Copper production with 114,500 tonnes in the third quarter, of which 52,500 tonnes were from QB. Production at QB continues to ramp-up and we expect to be operating at full throughput rates by the end of 2024.

    Zinc (000’s tonnes) 565 - 630
    Refined zinc (000’s tonnes) 240 - 250
    Sales Guidance – Q4 2024

    Unit Cost Guidance
